vllm.title: [Frontend] Log the maximum supported concurrency
vllm.author: AlpinDale
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR logs the maximum concurrency vLLM can handle, given all requests containing max_model_len number of tokens for each prompt. This is very useful for cases where you need to immediately get a rough estimate for your theoritical max batch size.
Currently calculating this as num_gpu_blocks * blo

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Optimize composite weight loading and fix EAGLE weight loading
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR consolidates and updates the weight loading logic to be more memory efficient, while also automatically detecting child modules and parameters. It also fixes issues with loading EAGLE models due to lm_head not existing in the weights.
This should resolve the CI failures related to LoRA and s

vllm.title: Add Dependabot configuration for GitHub Actions updates
vllm.author: EwoutH
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add a Dependabot configuration that checks once a week if the GitHub Actions are still using the latest version. If not, it opens a PR to update them.
It will open few PRs, since only major versions are specified (like v3), so only on a major release (like v4) it will update and open a PR. This way

vllm.title: [Hardware][CPU] Support AWQ for CPU backend
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR added a new ipex_quant configuration to support various weight-only quantization method with IPEX for the CPU backend.
AWQ is supported for now, a basic test is included.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] mypy: check vllm/entrypoints
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Resolve mypy warnings under vllm/entrypoints and turn on mypy
checks for this directory in format.sh and in CI.
part of issue #3680
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Update Dockerfile install+deploy image to ubuntu 22.04
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We need to build the vLLM wheel using ubuntu 20.04 in order to make a widely usable wheel (ref: #6517), but the final deployment image where we install the wheel can use a newer version with LTS. Several users have been reporting that it would be helpful for a variety of reasons (security, package v

vllm.title: [Core] Fix invalid args to _process_request
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I spotted this while working on expanding coverage with mypy. This
call to _process_request passed priority in the positional
argument location for prompt_adapter_request.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
previous approach of checking if the last line of the output contains a magic string is error-prone, if the process produces some logging, warning.
changing it to use the same technique as

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix Weight Loading Multiple GPU Test - Large Models
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The weight loading multiple gpu "large" test has been failing on main (link) due to the DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct model being too small for TP=2 with fp8 marlin. Let's try moving the model to the regular set of models to avoid A100.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Machete garbage results for some models (large K dim)
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Machete had a bug were the shape of the scales passed to the TMA was being set as:
make_shape(M, args.group_size, L)

when it should have been:
make_shape(M, scale_k, L)

this meant that when num_groups > group_size we would get garbage results (basically for for all groups with id > group_size the

vllm.title: [Core] Add an environment variable which needs to be set explicitly to allow BlockSpaceManagerV1
vllm.author: sroy745
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR introduces an environment variable that must be explicitly set to enable BlockSpaceManagerV1. This requirement informs users who have set use_v2_block_manager to False that BlockSpaceManagerV1 is being deprecated in favor of BlockSpaceManagerV2. As a result, users can test and transition to
